title: leontopodium subulatum
intro: Leontopodium subulatum, also known as Himalayan lavender or Himalayan edelweiss, is a flowering plant native to the Himalayan region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. It is said to have various health benefits, including reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and acting as an anti-inflammatory agent to alleviate pain and swelling. Leontopodium subulatum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
